commitMessage:
Bug 579169 - fix UI Freeze on Hover

PDE was even waiting for indexer to generate proposals in UI thread.

Change-Id: Id0e5ae5294d4bac5b2ee623d3b76b3cf82143a52
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/192362
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The change directly addresses a UI freeze to improve user experience.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support structural class transformations, including adding new fields, moving method bodies to new methods, and modifying constructor logic to pre-calculate and cache values. This requires more than expression or simple statement replacements.
summary: This commit fixes a UI freeze by pre-calculating completion proposals in a background thread and caching the result. This involves adding a new private field, moving existing logic from a getter method to a new private helper method, and then calling this helper from the constructor to store the result. The original getter then returns the cached value. This is a complex structural refactoring (field addition, method creation, method body relocation, constructor modification) that is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL, hence classified as RED. The impact on code improvement is high as it resolves a UI responsiveness bug, but its reusability as a direct DSL rule is very low due to its specificity and structural nature.

commitMessage:
Bug 579526: Remove duplicated code in Fix and FixCore classes

Change-Id: I930c8cc2fd7765517873f6a12ece67ed713ebf36
Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/192398
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
summary: This commit refactors internal JDT UI fix and correction classes by renaming `*Fix` to `*FixCore` and, in some cases, wrapping the result in `CleanUpFixWrapper.create()` or adding an explicit cast to an argument. These are direct 1:1 replacements of method calls, some involving class name changes in the receiver, wrapping the entire expression, or adding an explicit cast to an argument. This is an internal JDT API modernization and cleanup, fully expressible as multiple GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules.

irrelevantReason: This commit fixes a typo in a Javadoc comment, which is not a code transformation pattern suitable for TriggerPattern DSL rules. The DSL targets Java code logic and structure, not documentation text.
summary: This commit only fixes a minor typo within a Javadoc comment. This is a documentation change, not a code transformation that affects runtime behavior or logic, and therefore is not suitable for generalization into a TriggerPattern DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Bug 579612 - Regression: Fix for 578788 causes regression in NLS hover

- fix NLSStringHover.internalGetHoverInfo() logic to determine super
  class name of String field so it is fully qualified

Change-Id: I83e5bc17919e613c04890d1caf6d2f4035bd78b0
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/192549
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: Modifies internal JDT type resolution logic for NLS hover, shifting from Java Model to DOM bindings.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for contextual type-dependent API chain replacement and complex AST traversal with conditional logic, including changing the inferred type of a placeholder based on an 'instanceof' check and then using the new type's methods.
summary: This commit fixes a regression in JDT's NLS hover by refactoring internal type resolution logic within `NLSStringHover.java`. It shifts from using Java Model elements (`IType`, `IMember`) to DOM AST bindings (`ITypeBinding`, `IVariableBinding`) for determining the superclass of a String field's declaring type, and removes a `try-catch` block. This involves a complex restructuring of method call chains, `instanceof` checks, and conditional logic. The transformation is too complex for the current TriggerPattern DSL, which does not support such deep, type-dependent API chain replacements or conditional statement modifications.

commitMessage:
Bug 567254 - Wrong null check in CCPActionGroup 

Change-Id: Iceb3df47c94256740c92f0f6c29c926a8eba1a20
Signed-off-by: Paul Pazderski <paul-eclipse@ppazderski.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/169741
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Thomas  M??der <tmader@redhat.com>
categoryReason: Fixes a specific internal logic error where the wrong field was checked in a UI action handler.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the ability to match an expression based on subsequent or enclosing statements (e.g., 'replace `$expr1` only if it's the condition of an `if` statement whose body contains `$expr2`') to provide sufficiently specific context for the replacement. The current DSL cannot express such cross-statement contextual matching.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in `CCPActionGroup.java` where the `fCopyAction` variable was incorrectly checked in the `if` condition that controls the `CUT` action handler. The fix replaces `fCopyAction` with `fCutAction` in this specific conditional check. While a simple identifier replacement, the DSL currently cannot reliably match an expression based on the content of subsequent statements (i.e., 'replace `fCopyAction` only if the next line is `actionBars.setGlobalActionHandler(ActionFactory.CUT.getId(), fCutAction)`'). This contextual dependency makes the transformation too complex for the current pattern-matching capabilities of the DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 102996 - [type wizards] don't create main for inner types

- In new Class wizard page; add check if enclosing type is selected but
NOT static modifier, if so, the selection box for creating main method
is deselected and disabled

Change-Id: Ibdbf1d5bd0f448738751292455ff10fae08bf0c2
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/189716
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: Adds new UI-related conditional logic (an if-else block) to a wizard page to control main method creation.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for inserting new arbitrary multi-line `if-else` control flow blocks and multiple method invocation statements as a replacement. The current DSL is primarily for expression and simple statement replacements, not for complex code block insertions.
summary: This commit adds new conditional logic to `NewClassWizardPage.java` to prevent creating a `main` method for inner types that are not static. It inserts an entirely new `if-else` block with several method calls to enable/disable UI elements. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for expression and simple statement replacements, not for inserting new multi-line control flow structures with arbitrary nested statements. Therefore, this transformation is too complex for the current DSL capabilities.

commitMessage:
Bug 579588 - wrong setting of relevance for created type proposal

- fix JavaTypeCompletionProposalComputer.createTypeProposal() method
  to use the relevance passed to set relevance of the lazy completion
  proposals directly and not for the wrapped proposal

Change-Id: Ie7fd7b187309cd8e1e8d4db877b60e4853a3678e
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/192524
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: Fixes a bug related to internal JDT completion proposal logic.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support more complex structural rewrites, including inserting new local variable declarations and conditional statements based on existing code structure.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's `JavaTypeCompletionProposalComputer` where completion proposal relevance was incorrectly set. The fix involves moving a `setRelevance()` call from an inner object to a newly created wrapper object, conditionally within an `if-else` block. This is a structural code modification that introduces new local variables and changes control flow, which is beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 579635 - NPE in MatchLocator.findIndexMatches(MatchLocator.java:306)

SearchPattern.createPattern() can return null pattern if the input is
not valid. So don't pass null patterns down to the Search engine, it
doesn't like that.
categoryReason: Fixes a NullPointerException in internal JDT search components.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support inserting new 'if' statements and modifying control flow (e.g., adding 'continue;' or 'return;' statements) based on the result of an expression.
summary: This commit fixes a NullPointerException in JDT's search components by adding explicit null checks after `SearchPattern.createPattern()` calls. The fix involves inserting `if (pattern == null) { ... }` blocks with context-dependent actions (`continue`, `return 0`, `return null`). The TriggerPattern DSL is not capable of introducing new `if` statements or arbitrary control flow modifications, making this a RED transformation.

commitMessage:
Bug 99622 - [rename] Rename method misses ambiguously overridden method

- add null check to prevent NPE in TreeSet comparator
categoryReason: The commit introduces a specific fix for a NullPointerException, which falls under a general 'Bug Fix' category.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently does not support inserting entire 'if' control flow statements to wrap existing code, which is required to add a null check.
summary: This commit fixes Bug 99622 by adding a null check before calling `sameNodes.add(n)` in `RenameLinkedMode.java`. This prevents a NullPointerException if `NodeFinder.perform()` returns null. While adding null checks is a common safety pattern, the DSL cannot currently insert an entire 'if' statement to wrap an existing expression statement. This requires a significant extension to the DSL's structural modification capabilities.

commitMessage:
Fixes #12 : Bug 577317 - [17] Provide quick fixes to add permitted (#13)

* Fixes #12 : Bug 577317 - [17] Provide quick fixes to add permitted
class/interface for sealed class/interface

Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>

* Adding tests

Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The changes primarily involve modifying internal JDT UI code to support new Java language features (Sealed Classes), which is an internal refactoring.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support adding new method declarations, modifying method or constructor signatures (adding parameters), adding new switch cases, or complex multi-statement structural rewrites within method bodies. While a minor boolean expression change is present, the bulk of the commit is beyond the DSL's current capabilities.
summary: This commit introduces quick fixes for Java 17 Sealed Classes, allowing users to add permitted subtypes. This involves adding a new constant (`CREATE_NEW_SUB_TYPE`), introducing a new static method (`createNewTypeAsPermittedSubTypeProposal`), modifying existing constructors to include new parameters (`ITypeBinding superType`), updating complex UI wizard logic (`fillInWizardPageSuperTypes`), and dispatching to the new quick fix method in `QuickFixProcessor`. These changes are highly structural and involve modifications to method signatures, new method declarations, and intricate conditional logic that are not expressible using the current TriggerPattern DSL. This commit enhances an existing JDT UI cleanup plugin (`sandbox_junit_cleanup`) to support a new Java language feature.

commitMessage:
Moving text blocks destroys the contents indentation

- add check for multi-line string partition and don't indent line
  in IndentUtil.indentLine() method if within that partition
- fixes #21
categoryReason: The commit addresses a functional bug in the JDT UI editor's indentation logic for Text Blocks, a core internal component.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support inserting arbitrary control flow statements (like `if` blocks with `return` statements) and querying partition types. Current DSL is limited to expression and simple declaration replacements.
summary: This commit fixes an indentation bug when moving text blocks by adding a conditional check in `IndentUtil.indentLine()` that prevents indentation adjustments within multi-line string partitions. This is a structural change involving an `if` statement and a `return` statement, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based code transformations. It's classified as RED because it requires significant DSL extensions for statement-level control flow modifications.

commitMessage:
Combine declaration and init removing overriding assignment Bug 572440 (#27)

* Combine declaration and init removing overriding assignment Bug 572440

Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit introduces a new code cleanup related to combining variable declarations and their immediately following assignments, which is a common code style improvement.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need extensive extensions to handle this transformation. It requires complex control flow analysis (e.g., checking for intervening reads, side effects, passive initializers), detecting sequences of statements (declaration followed by assignment), and performing multi-statement structural rewrites (replacing an initializer, removing a statement, potentially moving a declaration to replace an assignment). This goes beyond simple expression replacement.
summary: This commit introduces a new internal JDT cleanup (`OverriddenAssignmentCleanUp`) that aims to combine variable declarations with their immediately following overriding assignments. The implementation involves complex AST traversal, flow analysis (e.g., checking for intervening reads, side effects), and conditional structural rewrites (replacing expressions, removing statements, and potentially moving the entire declaration). These operations are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level and simple declaration transformations. Therefore, this commit is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Fix indentation of Text Block (#33)

- fixes #30
- Text block indentation affects the final string and each line
  is considered in relation to the minimum indentation used so
  this relative indentation for lines that are more indented than
  the minimum must be preserved across indentation strategies
- added a class to store Text block info across multiple lines
  and pass this across calls in IndentAction when indenting lines
- changed IndentAction.getTextBlockIndentationString() to add any
  additional indentation for the line if it has indentation past
  the minimum for the text block
- added new IndentActionTest15
categoryReason: The commit fixes a bug in the JDT UI editor's indentation logic for Text Blocks, a core internal component.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support defining new inner classes, modifying method signatures (adding parameters), adding new methods, and performing complex algorithmic logic involving document parsing, indentation calculations, and conditional string manipulations. These are deep structural and algorithmic changes not supported by the current DSL.
summary: This commit fixes an indentation bug for Java Text Blocks by significantly refactoring internal JDT UI indentation logic. Changes include introducing a new inner class (`TextBlockInfo`), modifying multiple method signatures to accept `TextBlockInfo` and `minIndent` parameters, adding a new utility method (`getTextBlockMinIndent`), and implementing complex conditional logic for calculating and applying indentation within text blocks. These extensive structural and algorithmic changes are entirely beyond the scope of the current TriggerPattern DSL, classifying this commit as RED.

commitMessage:
[dogfooding] Apply new Combine declaration and init removing overriding (#35)

assignment cleanup on JDT UI

To ensure that new JDT cleanups are stable and work as intended we
should apply them to the Eclipse code base. This change uses the changes
from Bug 572440 on the jdt ui plug-in
categoryReason: This is a refactoring to improve code readability and remove redundant initializations.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs multi-statement pattern matching capabilities to identify a variable declared with an initial (often null or default) value and then immediately reassigned, consolidating these into a single declaration with the effective initialization. This involves control flow analysis for variable assignments.
summary: This commit applies a 'Combine declaration and init' cleanup. It identifies local variables that are declared (potentially with a `null` or default initializer) and then immediately assigned their actual value, combining these into a single declaration with initialization. This is a common and valuable cleanup but requires sophisticated multi-statement pattern matching and data-flow analysis (to ensure the intervening `null` initialization is truly redundant and the variable is not used before the 'actual' assignment) which is beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL's capabilities. Thus, it's classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Remove overridden assignment can result in two semicolons

- Fixes #36
- fix OverriddenAssignmentCleanUp.OverriddenAssignmentOperation
  to replace the ExpressionStatement with the new VarDeclaration
  statement instead of replacing the assignment which is just
  an expression
- disable removing of redundant semicolons in the CleanUpTest test
  so it verifies no additional semicolons are added
categoryReason: This commit fixes a bug within an existing JDT cleanup's implementation logic.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires modifying existing Java cleanup plugin logic, not a pattern for user code.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the internal `OverriddenAssignmentCleanUp` by adjusting which AST node is replaced during the rewrite (`overridingAssignment.getParent()` instead of `overridingAssignment`). This is a fix to the cleanup's own implementation logic, specifically how it interacts with the JDT `ASTRewrite` API. Such changes that correct the internal behavior of a cleanup, rather than defining a new transformation pattern for user code, cannot be generalized into a DSL rule. Thus, it's classified as RED.

commitMessage:
add integer and long to systemproperty cleanup (#26)

- reuse Boolean systemproperty cleanup to include Integer and Long
- rename cleanup checkbox to Boxed type
- add test cases to CleanUpTest including default values for boolean, long, integer
- fix ASTNodes.replaceAndRemoveNLS() method to use actual visited
  node contents from buffer rather than it's toString() method and
  modify replacement call string to add missing white-space

Co-authored-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: This is a refactoring of internal JDT UI preference constants and NLS messages.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support renaming local variables (e.g., `systemconstantsBooleanProperty` to `systemconstantsBoxedTypeProperty`) based on replaced values. While static field replacements (`CleanUpMessages.OLD_KEY` to `CleanUpMessages.NEW_KEY`) are generally possible, the combination with local variable renaming and the implicit dependency on a `.properties` file change (which the DSL cannot manage) makes this transformation too complex for direct DSL implementation.
summary: This commit renames internal NLS keys and constants within JDT UI cleanup preferences, specifically changing 'BooleanProperty' to 'BoxedTypeProperty'. This involves updating static field references in Java code and corresponding entries in `.properties` files, as well as renaming a local variable whose name is derived from the constant. The DSL can handle direct static field replacements but currently lacks capabilities for renaming local variables based on the replaced value or managing changes in resource files. Therefore, it's classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 514816 - [type wizards] Better validation for Enums and Annotations

- add override for typeNameChanged() method in NewEnumWizardPage to
  ensure that user does not specify type parameters for enum
categoryReason: The change adds complex validation logic to an Eclipse UI wizard, which is specific to UI interaction and not a general Java transformation.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL would need support for:
- Adding new helper methods with arbitrary Java code.
- Overriding existing methods and injecting complex conditional logic (if-else statements, return statements) into method bodies.
- More advanced structural AST manipulation beyond simple expression or statement replacement.
summary: This commit adds complex validation logic to the `NewEnumWizardPage` to prevent users from specifying type parameters for enum types. This involves adding a new private helper method (`getTypeNameWithoutParameters`) and overriding `typeNameChanged()` to inject an `if` block with validation and error reporting. These are structural changes (new method definition, overriding method with complex control flow and return statements) that are not directly expressible in the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 514816 - [type wizards] Better validation for Enums and Annotations

  - add override for typeNameChanged() method in NewAnnotationWizardPage
    to ensure that user does not specify type parameters for annotation
categoryReason: The change adds complex validation logic to an Eclipse UI wizard, which is specific to UI interaction and not a general Java transformation.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL would need support for:
- Adding new helper methods with arbitrary Java code.
- Overriding existing methods and injecting complex conditional logic (if-else statements, return statements) into method bodies.
- More advanced structural AST manipulation beyond simple expression or statement replacement.
summary: This commit adds complex validation logic to the `NewAnnotationWizardPage` to prevent users from specifying type parameters for annotation types. This change is almost identical in structure and complexity to Commit 0, involving adding a new private helper method and overriding `typeNameChanged()` to inject an `if` block with validation and error reporting. These are structural changes (new method definition, overriding method with complex control flow and return statements) that are not directly expressible in the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
NPE in ChainCompletionProposalComputer using CompletionProposalCollector

- fixes #45
- add check in ChainCompletionProposalComputer.initializeRequiredContext
  method
categoryReason: This is a bug fix specific to Eclipse JDT's internal completion proposal computer, preventing a NullPointerException.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL would need support for:
- Inserting arbitrary statement blocks (like 'if' statements) into a method body.
- Modifying control flow by adding 'return' statements within injected blocks.
summary: This commit fixes a NullPointerException in `ChainCompletionProposalComputer.initializeRequiredContext()` by adding an `if` statement to check if `ctx.getCompilationUnit()` is null before dereferencing it. This involves inserting a conditional control flow statement with a `return false;` statement, which is a structural change to the method's logic and is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL for expression-based transformations.

commitMessage:
This fix explicitly calls the active JavaEditor refresh of semantic highlighting to highlight newly added content of the editor(#50)

This fix explicitly calls the active JavaEditor refresh of semantic  highlighting to highlight newly added content of the editor, Fixes #25.

Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: This change reflects an internal API evolution within Eclipse JDT UI components, specifically updating how a constructor is called due to a signature change.
summary: This commit involves several changes: adding a new method to `JavaEditor`, modifying a constructor signature in `NewCUUsingWizardProposal`, and adding an `if` block with internal logic. While these structural changes are not directly DSL-implementable (RED), a key part of the commit is the modification of existing call sites for the `NewCUUsingWizardProposal` constructor. These calls are updated to include a new boolean argument, with its value (`true` or `false`) determined by the type of an existing argument (`IPackageFragment` or `IType`). This specific pattern of adding a new argument to a constructor call, conditionally based on existing arguments, is a direct 1:1 expression replacement and is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL with `instanceof` guards, making it a GREEN transformation.

commitMessage:
Fixes #62. [19] Allow JDT to target Java 19 in compiler preferences (#63)

Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: Updating internal compiler preference constants for new Java versions is a recurring configuration update pattern within JDT.
summary: This commit updates internal JDT UI compiler preferences to allow targeting Java 19. Specifically, it adds 'VERSION_19' to compliance arrays and updates a conditional check from `JavaCore.VERSION_18.equals(...)` to `JavaCore.VERSION_19.equals(...)`. This is a direct, automatable constant replacement within an expression, fitting existing internal JDT API modernization patterns.

commitMessage:
Coloring 'when' keyword in guarded pattern (#67)

Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: This commit adds new internal functionality to JDT (semantic highlighting for a new Java language feature) rather than transforming existing code or simply updating internal APIs.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires DSL extensions for adding new AST visitor methods and custom Java logic for semantic analysis and UI interaction (e.g., adding positions for highlighting).
summary: This commit adds a new AST visitor method to `SemanticHighlightingReconciler` to recognize and color the 'when' keyword within Java's Guarded Patterns (a Java 21+ feature). This involves adding new control flow, API calls to internal highlighting infrastructure, and custom logic, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based code transformations.

commitMessage:
Fix "Remove overridden assignment" cleanup preview

Fix the preview of the "Remove overridden assignment" cleanup (in the "Unnecessary Code" tab) when the option "Move declaration if necessary" is not enabled.
categoryReason: Fixes an internal preview generation logic bug within an existing JDT cleanup, not a general user-facing code transformation.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL is designed for transforming user code, not for modifying the internal string literals used by a cleanup to generate code previews. While a string literal replacement is possible, the context and intent (fixing an internal cleanup bug) make it unsuitable for a general DSL rule.
summary: This commit fixes an internal bug in the preview generation of the 'Remove overridden assignment' cleanup. It changes a string literal used to construct the preview code from `"long time = 0;\n"` to `"long time;\n"`. This is a bug fix for an existing cleanup's internal logic, not a generalizable code transformation pattern for user code. Such a specific fix within internal cleanup infrastructure is not suitable for a reusable TriggerPattern DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Cannot run JDT cleanups for multiple projects in project explorer

- fixes #34
- support IProject selection in CleanUpAction.isEnabled() and
  CleanUpAction.getCompilationUnits()
categoryReason: Fixes a functional bug in an Eclipse JDT UI action by adding new conditional logic and variable reassignments.
languageChangeNeeded: The TriggerPattern DSL does not support inserting new `if` statements or conditional control flow. It primarily handles replacement of existing expressions, statements, or annotations. This change requires adding new structural elements to the code.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in `CleanUpAction` that prevented JDT cleanups from running on multiple projects selected in the Project Explorer. The fix involves inserting new `if` statements to check if a selected element is an `IProject` and then converting it to an `IJavaProject` using `JavaCore.create()`. This transformation requires adding new conditional logic and variable reassignments, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
While loop to foreach loop conversion (#55)

Add while statement support to enhanced-for cleanup

- support changing while loops using iterators into enhanced for loops similar to what is done
  with for loops using iterators
- add this support under existing enhanced for loop cleanup option
- add new form of AST visitor that supports lambda expressions (LambdaASTVisitor) and
  add new classes (HelperVisitor and HelperVisitorProvider) that makes it simple to add a visit method
  for a particular AST construct
- add new tests to CleanUpTest1d8

Co-authored-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit's core change is to introduce a new cleanup type focused on converting specific loop structures.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support complex structural transformations that involve recognizing iterator patterns (while with hasNext/next), extracting the collection, inferring the element type, and rewriting the entire loop construct into an enhanced for-loop. This would likely require a powerful FixFunction in Java, or new DSL constructs for statement-level structural transformation with type inference and variable capture/injection.
summary: This commit introduces a new Java-based cleanup, `UseIteratorToForLoopCleanUp`, designed to convert `while` loops that iterate over collections using `Iterator` into enhanced `for` loops. While the underlying concept of loop conversion is highly relevant for code modernization, the provided diff primarily shows the `AbstractCleanUp` wrapper class and a minor UI label update in the `CleanUpMessages.properties` file. The actual transformation logic (converting the `while` loop's structure and extracting the collection/element types) is implemented in an imperative Java class (`UseIteratorToForLoopCleanUpCore.java`, not in the diff). This is a complex structural rewrite that involves recognizing an iterator pattern, inferring types, and rewriting statements, which is currently beyond the capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for direct pattern-to-replacement rules. It would require significant DSL extensions or a dedicated `FixFunction` implemented in Java, classifying this as a RED transformation for the DSL.

commitMessage:
Inline module info question #59 (#91)

This change adds the module-info file name field and related fields to the Java Project creation wizard. Fixes #59 

Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
irrelevantReason: This commit introduces new UI components, internal logic, and refactors an existing feature's implementation (from action-based to direct utility calls). It involves adding new methods, new classes, complex conditional logic, UI setup, and replacing entire functional blocks. There are no simple pattern-to-replacement code transformations that can be generalized into a reusable TriggerPattern DSL rule.
summary: This commit introduces new functionality for creating `module-info.java` files within the Java Project creation wizard. The changes are predominantly structural (adding a new inner class `ModuleGroup` with complex UI and validation logic), involve UI layout updates, NLS message additions, and a significant refactoring of how the `module-info` file is created (from an asynchronous `CreateModuleInfoAction` to direct calls to `InfoFilesUtil.createInfoJavaFile()`). These types of architectural, UI-related, and complex logical rewrites are not suitable for generalization into pattern-based TriggerPattern DSL rules. Therefore, this commit is classified as NOT_APPLICABLE for DSL mining.

commitMessage:
Ignore leading white-space when finding element at offset

- modify SelectionConverter.getElementAtOffset() to by-pass white
  space at beginning of ITextSelection so that enclosing element
  will be correct and not find the parent element
- fixes https://bugs.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=67077
categoryReason: The commit fixes a bug in an internal Eclipse JDT UI component.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for inserting new local variable declarations, conditional statements (if), and loops (while) within method bodies. The current DSL primarily supports expression replacements and simpler statement-level rewrites, not arbitrary control flow insertion.
summary: The commit fixes a bug in `SelectionConverter.getElementAtOffset()` by introducing logic to ignore leading whitespace in a text selection. This involves adding a new local variable (`whiteSpaceOffset`), an `if` block, and a `while` loop to compute the offset, which is then added to the argument of `input.getElementAt()`. The current TriggerPattern DSL cannot express the insertion of new local variables, `if` statements, or `while` loops into existing code, making this transformation impossible to implement directly with the current DSL capabilities. Thus, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage: Default Execution Environment for Y build is 9 (fixes #131 ) (#133)
categoryReason: The changes are specific to the internal logic and configuration of Eclipse JDT UI, fitting existing JDT Internal Refactoring patterns.
summary: This commit refines the default execution environment selection logic and updates the default Java version in the New Java Project wizard. Specifically, it changes a string comparison from `endsWith()` to `equals()` for execution environment compliance, enforcing a stricter match. Additionally, it updates the hardcoded default execution environment from 'JavaSE-1.7' to 'JavaSE-11'. Both are internal changes to the Eclipse JDT UI codebase, aiming to fix the default EE. These are direct 1:1 expression replacements, fully implementable as two distinct TriggerPattern DSL rules with `inClass` and `inMethod` guards, and are categorized as JDT Internal Refactoring.

commitMessage:
[19] Quickfix for "you are using preview feature" causes compilation error (#153)

Fixes #137.

Signed-off-by: Noopur Gupta <noopur_gupta@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The change fixes a bug within an existing internal JDT UI quick-fix component.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs the capability to insert new conditional `if` blocks containing arbitrary statements, including `return` statements, and to reference newly introduced local variables (implicitly or explicitly). Currently, it only supports expression replacements or method annotation additions.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's `SuppressWarningsSubProcessor` where the 'preview feature' quickfix caused a compilation error when a `SingleVariableDeclaration` was part of a `PatternInstanceofExpression`. The fix involves inserting a new `if` statement with a `return` statement within an existing `switch` case. This is a structural code modification that introduces new control flow and local logic. The current TriggerPattern DSL is designed for expression-level and simple statement-level replacements/additions, not for inserting complex conditional blocks with `return` statements. Therefore, this change is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Detect/avoid illegal combination of --release and --add-exports (#150)

- raise error when attempting --release with existing addExports
- disable addExports button when --release is active
categoryReason: The commit involves several internal JDT UI refactorings related to build path configuration and preference handling, specifically addressing interactions between compiler options.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL needs to support:
1.  Modifying method modifiers (e.g., `static boolean` to `public static boolean`).
2.  Changing method signatures (adding or removing parameters, including parameter types).
3.  Introducing new local variables and then using them in subsequent rule replacements (e.g., `enableAddExport`).
4.  Inserting complex new conditional `if` blocks and `if-else` structures, potentially involving new API calls (`new Status(...)`, `MessageFormat.format(...)`).
Currently, the DSL is limited to expression-level replacements, simple statement additions, or method annotation changes. The patterns in this commit are too structural and context-dependent for direct DSL implementation.
summary: This commit detects and avoids an illegal combination of the `--release` and `--add-exports` compiler options within JDT UI's `ComplianceConfigurationBlock` and `ModuleDependenciesAdapter`. It introduces new NLS messages, adds complex conditional logic to validate preferences, modifies method signatures (e.g., changing `isJREContainer` to `public`, adding parameters to `updateButtonEnablement` and `getConfiguredDetails`), and introduces new local variables (`enableAddExport`) whose values influence subsequent API calls. These changes are highly structural, involve modifying method visibility/signatures, inserting control flow (new `if` blocks), and referencing newly introduced local variables in method arguments. Such complex, context-dependent architectural changes are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-based and simple statement transformations. Therefore, this commit is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Prevent moving initializer in error cases. Fixes #136 (#147)

Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>

Checks two things and prevents moving the initializer if true:

1. Whether a variable is reassigned in a statement that may have sub-
statements (like an if statement)
2. Whether the initializer uses variables that are not define at the
place the initializer would be move up to.
categoryReason: This commit directly addresses and fixes incorrect behavior within an existing JDT cleanup plugin.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires advanced semantic analysis (variable reassignment, variable scope/definition in arbitrary target locations), complex AST traversal, and programmatic AST string manipulation (ASTNode.createStringPlaceholder, ICompilationUnit.getBuffer().getText). These go beyond simple pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the `OverriddenAssignmentCleanUp` by introducing complex semantic checks for variable reassignment and scope validity, and by using programmatic AST string manipulation. These changes involve deep AST analysis (e.g., `VarDefinitionsUsesVisitor`, custom AST visitors for variable definition checks) and complex AST rewrite operations using `createStringPlaceholder` and `ICompilationUnit.getBuffer().getText`, which are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations. The modifications are internal to the cleanup's Java implementation, not a user-facing code transformation pattern.

commitMessage:
Move trailing comments with initializer. Fixes #56 (#128)

* Move trailing comments with initializer. Fixes #56

Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>

Co-authored-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: This commit directly addresses and fixes incorrect behavior within an existing JDT cleanup plugin, specifically related to comment preservation during refactoring.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires advanced AST manipulation for comment preservation (e.g., custom `TargetSourceRangeComputer`), detailed line-number based analysis, and programmatic AST string manipulation. These are beyond the current DSL capabilities for pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the `OverriddenAssignmentCleanUp` related to moving trailing comments. It introduces complex logic involving line number calculations (`doesNotShareLines`), custom `TargetSourceRangeComputer` for managing source ranges with comments, and extensive use of `ICompilationUnit.getBuffer().getText()` and `ASTRewrite.createStringPlaceholder()` for precise text-based AST manipulation. These changes are deeply integrated into the Java-based cleanup implementation for comment preservation and are not expressible as pattern-based TriggerPattern DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Fix rename regression with renaming overridden interface method (#163)

* Fix rename regression with renaming overridden interface method

- fixes #158
- add check in RippleMethodFinder2.findAllRippleMethods() to bypass
  an issue where search cannot find the original method in the
  compilation unit
- fix logic in RenameLinkedMode.start() so that the name node isn't
  added twice and the additional methods found by RippleMethodFinder2
  are processed correctly
- add new test to RenameVirtualMethodInClassTests and enable some
  tests that weren't being run
categoryReason: This commit fixes a regression in JDT's internal refactoring engine by changing the underlying collection type and its usage.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires semantic understanding of collection types (Set vs. List) and their impact on logic (uniqueness, order). The DSL is not designed to refactor collection instantiation and usage based on implicit behavioral changes; it's focused on expression/statement level replacements. A DSL extension would need to understand and suggest changes to local variable types and subsequent access patterns, potentially for a whole method body.
summary: This commit fixes a regression in JDT's internal rename refactoring by replacing a `TreeSet` (implicitly `sameNodes`) with an `ArrayList` (`nodes`) and updating all its usage. This change alters the collection's behavior (e.g., handling of duplicates, preservation of order) and is a structural refactoring of local variable type and data flow. The TriggerPattern DSL is not capable of understanding or transforming such semantic changes in collection usage and variable declarations. This requires a deeper type system and flow analysis that is beyond expression-level pattern matching.

commitMessage:
JavaSourceHover support for TextBlock. Fixes #164 (#165)

Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The commit adds support for a new Java language feature (Text Blocks) to an internal JDT UI component (JavaSourceHover), involving new code and complex logic.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs support for adding new method declarations, inserting complex multi-line if-else-return blocks, and generating code that involves AST parsing and document partitioning logic. Current DSL is limited to expression-level and simple declaration/annotation transformations.
summary: This commit introduces support for Text Blocks (Java 15+) in JDT's `JavaSourceHover`. It involves adding new private helper methods (`getTextBlockHoverInfo`, `isTextBlock`, `getProject`) and modifying existing control flow to conditionally call these new methods. The core changes are structural additions of new methods and complex multi-line `if-else` logic, including AST parsing, document partitioning, and conditional method invocations. These types of changes are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which is primarily designed for pattern-based expression replacements rather than arbitrary code generation or complex control flow insertion. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Add parameter name code mining support for records. (#149)

- Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/148
- Use IMethodBinding#getParameterNames() to support parameter name code
  mining for records
- Simply pass the parameter names at the call site and adjust methods
  accordingly for JavaMethodParameterCodeMining constructor &
  skipParameterNameCodeMining(..)
- Add testcase for record constructor to ParameterNamesCodeMiningTest
- Add JavaProjectHelper.addRTJar_17(..)

Signed-off-by: Roland Grunberg <rgrunber@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit focuses on internal JDT code, specifically for enhancing code mining features related to method parameter names, especially for records.
languageChangeNeeded: The core changes involve modifying method and constructor signatures (e.g., replacing 'IMethod' with 'String[]' array for parameter names), introducing new helper methods to encapsulate logic, and refactoring complex internal control flow. The DSL is designed for expression-level and statement-level pattern matching and replacement, not for modifying method/constructor signatures, introducing new internal classes/methods, or rewriting complex internal control flow logic that involves custom AST visitors. These changes are structural and architectural in nature, making them beyond the current DSL's capabilities.
summary: This commit refactors internal JDT UI code mining logic to support parameter name display for records. It involves significant internal architectural changes, such as modifying method signatures in `JavaMethodParameterCodeMining` and `CalleeJavaMethodParameterVisitor` (e.g., changing parameter types from `IMethod` to `String[]`), introducing new helper methods like `collectParameterNameCodeMinings` to encapsulate logic, and simplifying internal method bodies by removing `try-catch` blocks and direct `IMethod` calls. These structural and architectural refactorings, including method signature changes and complex control flow modifications, cannot be expressed with the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Add new buttons to Cleanup preference pages (#162)

* Add new buttons to Cleanup preference pages

- add new Select All, Deselect All buttons for current tab page
- add Set Defaults and Reset Profile buttons for all pages
- fixes #119
categoryReason: The primary goal of this commit is to add new buttons and functionality to Eclipse JDT cleanup preference pages, which is a UI-specific enhancement.
languageChangeNeeded: This commit introduces new UI components (buttons, groups) and modifies existing UI layout programmatically. It involves overriding `createButtonsForButtonBar` and `createContents`, creating new `Button` instances, configuring their layout, attaching `SelectionListener`s (using lambda expressions), and defining new abstract methods in `CleanUpTabPage` (like `setDefaults`, `resetValues`, `doSetAll`). The DSL is focused on code transformation via pattern matching and replacement of existing code structures, not on generating new UI elements, dynamically adding event listeners, or defining new abstract methods and their concrete implementations. These are fundamental structural changes to the UI framework that are beyond the DSL's current capabilities.
summary: This commit adds 'Select All', 'Deselect All', 'Set Defaults', and 'Reset Profile' buttons to the JDT cleanup preference pages. This involves significant UI code, including overriding `createButtonsForButtonBar`, adding new button creations, wiring up event listeners with lambda expressions, and introducing new abstract methods in `CleanUpTabPage`. These are structural additions to the Eclipse UI framework that cannot be expressed using the TriggerPattern DSL, which is designed for code transformation, not UI generation or new method definitions.

commitMessage:
Don't move up initializer when using undefined vars. Fixes #38 (#64)

* Don't move up initializer when using undefined vars. Fixes #38

Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>

Don't move up the initializer of an overridden assignment if it uses
identifiers that are defined after the variable declaration.

Co-authored-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The main change addresses a bug in an existing JDT cleanup (`OverriddenAssignmentCleanUp`) by preventing incorrect code movement.
languageChangeNeeded: The core of this commit is a complex bug fix for an existing JDT cleanup. It introduces an anonymous inner class (`DeclarationChecker`) that extends `ASTVisitor` to perform detailed AST traversal and analysis. This visitor checks for identifier declarations within specific source ranges (`start`, `end`) to determine if moving an initializer would introduce undefined variable references. The result of this analysis then modifies a boolean flag (`canMoveUp`) that controls the cleanup's behavior. The DSL cannot define new classes (even anonymous ones), implement custom AST visitors, or perform such intricate semantic analysis and control flow modifications based on deep AST traversal. This is fundamentally about fixing the internal logic of a Java-based cleanup.
summary: This commit fixes Bug #38 in `OverriddenAssignmentCleanUp` by introducing an AST visitor to prevent moving up initializers that depend on variables defined later in the code. This involves adding complex internal logic, including an anonymous inner class with custom AST visiting, to perform deep semantic analysis of variable definitions and usages. Such structural additions and advanced semantic analysis are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern matching and replacement. While there's a minor helper method usage change, the core bug fix is RED for DSL implementation.

commitMessage:
improve Dark Theme on Windows eclipse-platform/.github#27 (#126)

Using CTabFolder and CTabItem, instead of TabFolder/TabItem, improves
the look when in Dark Theme; the background of the tab no longer has a
non-dark background.

This relates to multiple commits to fix:

[Windows][Dark theme] Multiple preference pages use tab widgets, that
are not dark theme aware eclipse-platform/.github#27
categoryReason: The commit modernizes the usage of SWT TabFolder/TabItem API to their CTabFolder/CTabItem counterparts within Eclipse UI, which is a common Eclipse API migration pattern.
summary: This commit improves the dark theme on Windows by migrating `TabFolder` and `TabItem` usage to `CTabFolder` and `CTabItem` in `BuildPathsBlock.java` and `ModuleDependenciesPage.java`. This is a systematic replacement of SWT widget types, including type references, constructor invocations, and casts. These are direct 1:1 API replacements that enhance UI aesthetics and adhere to modern SWT practices. The transformation is fully expressible as a set of GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules, as the DSL supports type replacements and constructor invocations. The addition of `folder.setSelection(0)` is a single statement insertion directly related to the new widget and can be handled as a minor side-effect or separate transformation, but the core type migration is the dominant pattern.

commitMessage:
Use platform line separator in hover. Fixes #85 (#86)

Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>
categoryReason: Replaces a hardcoded newline string literal with System.lineSeparator() for platform independence and readability.
summary: Replaces a hardcoded double newline string literal (`"\n\n"`) with the platform-independent `System.lineSeparator()` repeated twice within a string concatenation. This is a direct expression replacement, improving portability and readability, and is fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Whitespaceissue (#177)

* Fixes #175

- for all white-space selection, don't set a white-space offset
  in SelectionConverter.getElementAtOffset()
- also add length check to avoid IOOBException
categoryReason: Fixes an IndexOutOfBoundsException in internal JDT UI code by adding a length check to a while loop condition and introducing a new if-statement block.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support inserting new statement blocks and complex structural rewrites of control flow, not just expression replacements. While a single expression in the `while` loop condition could be replaced, the addition of a new `if` statement block is not supported.
summary: Fixes an `IndexOutOfBoundsException` in an internal `SelectionConverter` method by adding a length check to a `while` loop condition and introducing a new `if` block. The `while` condition change is an expression replacement, but the addition of a new `if` statement block is not supported by the current DSL, classifying it as RED.

commitMessage: Make new cleanup tab page buttons default to noops to help Xtend (#179)
categoryReason: Changes method declarations from abstract to concrete (removes 'abstract' keyword, adds empty method body) in an internal JDT UI class. The DSL does not support modifying method modifiers or adding method bodies.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs explicit support for modifying method modifiers (e.g., adding/removing 'abstract') and adding empty or simple method bodies to method declarations.
summary: Removes the `abstract` modifier from three method declarations and adds empty method bodies, effectively making them concrete no-op methods. This is an internal JDT UI refactoring. The DSL does not support direct modification of method modifiers or adding method bodies in this manner, classifying it as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 579578: use CleanUpFixWrapper instead of Wrapper CleanupFix classes (#92)

Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>
categoryReason: Refactors internal JDT cleanup logic by renaming classes and wrapping method calls with `CleanUpFixWrapper.create()`. The wrapper often takes a lambda expression with context-dependent arguments, which is too complex for the current DSL to generate reliably.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs robust support for generating complex lambda expressions in replacements, especially when they introduce new parameters and capture variables from the outer scope, and for advanced structural refactorings like wrapping method calls with new constructs that include lambdas.
summary: Refactors internal JDT `Fix` classes (e.g., `TypeParametersFix` to `TypeParametersFixCore`) and their usage. It involves class renames and wrapping existing method calls with `CleanUpFixWrapper.create()`, often passing a lambda expression as an argument. The generation of such complex lambda expressions in the replacement is beyond the current DSL's capabilities, making the commit RED. Simple class renames are possible but insufficient to capture the full change.
